title: Treatment of VL_CPU_RELAX on unknown architectures
body:
A portion of `include/verilatedos.h` attempts to define useful (and architecture-dependent) values of the VL_CPU_RELAX macro.
But the default case, for unknown architectures, is to error.

The documented reason for this macro is efficiency on SMT CPUs.  Is that reason to block building on an unknown architecture?
This condition was hit for Verilator 5.004 on Debian auto-builders for mipsel, mips64el, and s390x.

https://buildd.debian.org/status/package.php?p=verilator
